channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Live: Israel strikes Beirut for the first time since Hezbollah ceasefire as Iran weighs up peace proposal
item.description: The strikes come as Iran weighs up a peace deal put forward by the US, with Donald Trump saying the war could "be at an end". Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Israel has carried out strikes on Beirut for the first time in weeks.

item.title: 'I'll show no mercy': Charity founder faces 'tickle torture' allegations
item.description: More victims of former Order of Australia recipient Cody Schaeffer have spoken to the ABC about his actions.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie and Talissa Siganto

media:group.media:description: Cody Schaeffer pleaded guilty in March to assaulting five teenagers at his home between 2021 and 2023.

item.title: Children in Melbourne's inner west at higher risk of asthma hospital visits
item.description: There are renewed calls for government action after another study finds children in Melbourne's inner west are over-represented in asthma-related hospital visits.
item.dc:creator: Leanne Wong

media:group.media:description: Children like Sylvia Barbadonis's sons and daughter have an increased risk of needing hospital treatment for asthma, a study has found.

item.title: What will become of the Victoria Barracks? Communities have their say
item.description: Veteran, housing, heritage and community organisations are urging the government to put public needs first as dozens of defence sites get prepped for sale.
item.dc:creator: Annika Burgess

media:group.media:description: Community groups are concerned about the planned sale of Victoria Barracks in Melbourne (pictured) and Sydney.

item.title: This island was once ruled by Australia. Now, it could become the world's newest country
item.description: A tropical archipelago just 1,500 kilometres from Australia is readying to become the world's newest country. But as Bougainville nears independence, memories of a bloody past are coming back to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Marian Faa, Gabriella Marchant, Theckla Gunga, Cordelia Brown, and Steven Viney

media:group.media:description: The Panguna mine, once operated by Australian mining giant Rio Tinto, as seen from the ocean near Arawa, Bougainville’s former capital.

item.title: Does this generation have it harder? The answer is complicated
item.description: Next week's budget will have the generation gap at its heart. But nailing down exactly what that gap is can be difficult. Here's what the evidence says.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crowley

media:group.media:description: Australia's generation gap is in focus in the lead-up to the federal budget.

item.title: Leader who overcame One Nation's rise warns against dismissing its voters
item.description: Nearly three decades after One Nation's shock breakthrough in Queensland, Pauline Hanson's party is again within reach of a significant political victory.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: One Nation has enjoyed a significant bump in support at the state level, according to polling.

item.title: Would you rather hand over $300 a month to your bank or the government?
item.description: As the average mortgage borrower confronts another $100 a month on top of their already inflated repayments, lots of people are asking if there is a better way to contain inflation than simply raising interest rates. It turns out there might be.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: A borrower with a fairly typical $700,000 mortgage has seen a $317 increase to their monthly repayments with three rate rises this year.

item.title: Is Richard Dawkins right about AI?
item.description: Is AI conscious? Richard Dawkins is asking the wrong question.
item.dc:creator: Alan Kohler

media:group.media:description: Atheist and author Richard Dawkins recently concluded that AI was conscious.

item.title: Albanese's budget won't be derailed by Trump's declarations
item.description: It's clear that whatever this unpredictable US president says, the Albanese government isn't expecting a return to normal oil flows any time soon.
item.dc:creator: David Speers

media:group.media:description: It's clear that whatever this unpredictable US president says, the Albanese government isn't expecting a return to normal oil flows any time soon.

item.title: ISIS-linked families to arrive in Australia today after years in Syria
item.description: Australian authorities are preparing for the arrival of 13 women and children linked to Islamic State fighters, expected to land in the country from Syria today.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason and Pablo Viñales

media:group.media:description: A group boarded a connecting flight from Doha.

item.title: How Iain escaped Australia's insecure, unaffordable rental nightmare
item.description: What if the sudden improvement in Iain's life, and the extra $600 a month in his pocket, can be traced back to a single policy decision made by a federal minister in the 1980s?
item.dc:creator: Rhiannon Stevens

media:group.media:description: Iain on the balcony of his rental cooperative apartment in Melbourne.

item.title: Dreaming of an escape? Try these bucket-list Aussie road trips
item.description: Whether you want to explore the red desert or picnic by a waterfall, an Aussie road trip awaits.
item.dc:creator: Shiloh Payne

media:group.media:description: There's no limit to how grand a road trip can be.

item.title: Can you identify Aussie movies from a single frame?
item.description: Think you know your Moulin Rouge from your Muriel's Wedding? Take our Australian movie quiz to prove you're the king of The Castle.
item.dc:creator: Velvet Winter

media:group.media:description: Toni Collette as Muriel in Muriel's Wedding.

item.title: Pussy Riot joins anti-Russia smoke bomb protest at Venice Biennale
item.description: Among those in the crowed were members of the Russian punk group Pussy Riot, who attended protests against the country's participation in the Italian art show.

media:group.media:description: The protests were prompted by Russia's participation in the Italian art show.

item.title: Iran reviews US proposal as Trump says war could 'be at an end'
item.description: Iran reviews a new peace-deal proposal from the US, which President Donald Trump says could end the war, but an Iranian official describes it as an "American wish list".
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump said bombing would resume at a "much higher level" if Iran did not agree to a deal.

item.title: Ted Turner, pioneering founder of CNN, dead at 87
item.description: Turner revolutionised television news when he launched the world's first 24-hour news channel in 1980.

media:group.media:description: Ted Turner's decision to found CNN was initially mocked, but the network would reshape the way audiences consumed news.

item.title: Australian government 'deciding factor' in departure of ISIS-linked families from Syria
item.description: The Syrian government says Australia was the ultimate decision-maker in the delay of a group of four women and nine children with links to Islamic State leaving Syria for Australia.
item.dc:creator: Bridget Rollason

media:group.media:description: A larger group of Australians attempted to leave the Al Roj refugee camp for home in February.

item.title: Murder trial hears couple 'looked madly in love' before violent death
item.description: Braden Jentian's defence lawyer has told jurors his partner was killed by "something or someone else" before she was found dead on a Katherine street in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Barrett Peters

media:group.media:description: Rebecca Everitt told the jury the couple spent several hours drinking at bars around Katherine on the day she died.

item.title: Olympic stadium at Victoria Park cleared by environmental department
item.description: The proposal for a new stadium to be built at Victoria Park and an aquatic centre in Spring Hill has been declared "not a controlled action" by the federal environmental department.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: The stadium for the 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games at Victoria Park has been cleared by the environmental department.

item.title: 'Terrible' cockroach infestation driving residents out of Perth housing block
item.description: Residents are seeking to break their leases as a cockroach infestation at an inner-west public housing unit spreads.
item.dc:creator: Kenith Png

media:group.media:description: The infestation started at a public housing unit.

item.title: Canary Islands leader doesn't want hantavirus-hit cruise ship to dock
item.description: A luxury cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak and marooned for days off the coast of Cape Verde with close to 150 people on board is on its way to Spain's Canary Islands, where a regional leader says it is not welcome.

media:group.media:description: Health workers in protective gear evacuate patients from the MV Hondius cruise ship at a port in Praia, Cape Verde on Wednesday.

item.title: Ministers insist 'no conflict of interest' before declaring relationship
item.description: Queensland ministers Tim Mander and Amanda Camm have fielded questions about their relationship and timeline.
item.dc:creator: Jack McKay

media:group.media:description: Tim Mander and Amanda Camm have faced more questions about their relationship.

item.title: Mother's warning after cake-decorating dust leaves toddler in coma
item.description: The mother of a Queensland toddler who is in an induced coma after inhaling a dust used for decorating cakes is calling for similar products to be pulled from shelves.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Williams and Jordan Bissell

media:group.media:description: Dustin Wildman is in an induced coma after inhaling dust used to decorate cakes.

item.title: Exercise physiologists demand an end to 'discriminatory' health tax
item.description: The peak body for exercise physiologists says its members are being discriminated against with "unfair" taxes that other health services do not have to pay.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: Tim Dogue says the GST on exercise physiology services is unfair and discriminatory.
